MATER MISERICORDIAE HOSPITAL (2008) 169 CAL.APP.4TH 357 MERITS EXTENSIVE QUOTATION: (1) "MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT MAY BE BROUGHT ONLY UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 437C AND IN ACCORDANCE WITH ITS REQUIREMENTS;" (2) CCP 437C(F)(1) PERMITS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MOTIONS REGARDING "NEWLY DISCOVERED FACTS OR CIRCUMSTANCES," BUT "[S]UCH A MOTION IS A "NEW SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION" THAT MUST COMPLY WITH CCP 437C'S REQUIREMENTS; AND (3) CCP 1008 IS NOT TO THE CONTRARY; IT "DOES NOT PURPORT TO AUTHORIZE A NEW SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION THAT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS FOR SUCH MOTIONS SET OUT IN CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 437C")(EMPHASIS IN ORIGINAL).) Off Calendar Nov-16-2018 Text Ruling

RULING - The matter is Discussed. The Court has received and email the Defendats submit to the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT's Motion For Renewed Request For Summary Adjudication On Issue I.

Defendant The Travelers Indemnity Company of Connecticut's "motion for renewed request for summary adjudication on issue 1" is DENIED because it does not comply with the requirements of CCP 437c. Management, Inc. v. Mater Misericordiae Hospital (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 357 merits extensive quotation: (1) "Motions for summary judgment may be brought only under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c and in accordance with its requirements;" (2) CCP 437c(f)(1) permits summary adjudication motions regarding "newly discovered facts or circumstances," but "[s]uch a motion is a "new summary judgment motion" that must comply with CCP 437c's requirements; and (3) CCP 1008 is not to the contrary; it "does not purport to authorize a new summary judgment motion that does not comply with the requirements for such motions set out in Code of Civil Procedure section 437c")(emphasis in original).) (Id. at 368.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Diane K. Fraziers Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-17-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Terrence Coleman, Esq. 415-433-8000 of Pillbury Coleman for Plaintiff DON TUCKER, MARTIN AGUILAR.
Bruce Celebrezze, Esq. 415-365-9800 pf Clyde & Co. for Defendant Diane K. Frazier & The Travelers Indemnity Company Of Connecticut.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant Argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT Diane K. Frazier's Motion For Summary Judgment.

Defendant Diane Frazier's motion for summary judgment on the sole cause of action for negligent misrepresentation alleged against her in the complaint filed by plaintiff Don Tucker is denied. Per California law, an insured has a claim against an adjuster who is employed by an insurer based on a misrepresentation made by the adjuster. Ms. Frazier's asserted statement that "there is no coverage for this loss, as the work was taking place at a residence in San Francisco and there is a full residential exclusion on that policy" is an actionable representation of fact because it is a provably false statement. There is a triable dispute whether Ms. Frazier's statement was false because the excavation of the swimming pool is not encompassed within the residential exclusion. Mr. Tucker's objections to the evidence submitted by Ms. Frazier with her reply papers are sustained. There are also triable disputes whether 1) Ms. Frazier had reasonable grounds for believing her statement was true; 2) Ms. Frazier intended for Mr. Tucker to rely on her statement; 3) Mr. Tucker actually and reasonably relied on the statement; and 4) Mr. Tucker suffered harm as a result of his reliance on the statement. Liberally construing the evidence in favor of Mr. Tucker and drawing all inferences in his favor, there is evidence showing: a) Ms. Frazier made the statement within hours of receiving a claim without review and approval of her supervisor contrary to Travelers' policies; b) Ms. Frazier knew her statement would be communicated to Mr. Tucker and would be relied on by him in his business decisions; and c) Mr. Tucker purchased additional insurance because of the statement. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for Mr. Tucker is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Don Tucker To Provide Amended Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Terrence J. Coleman, Pillsbury, appearing for Plaintiff Don Tucker, Telephone(415) 433-8000;
Dean McElroy, Clyde & Co US LLP, appearing for Defendnat Travelers Indemnity Company of Connecticut, Telephone (415) 365-9898;
Court Reporter: Jaime Hopp, CSR #13751, Telephone (925)759-0770, Email Jaimehopp@gmail.com

Ruling: The Court Modifies its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT'S Motion To Compel Don Tucker To Provide Amended Responses To Discovery is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part.

Traveler's Motion to Compel Amended Responses to Special Interrogatories Set One Nos. 5-8 is Granted. Defendant Traveler's Separate Statement in Support fails to set forth as to each Special Interrogatory the reasons why the requested information is relevant and discoverable. Rather, Traveler's Separate Statement recites general principles relating to liberal discovery and assumes its entitlement to "a more complete picture of Tucker's work and provision of equipment at the 2901 Broadway project" (Traveler's Separate Statement, page 7 lines 3-4) without ever explaining why such information is relevant here. Rule of Court 3.1345 (c) (3). Without that showing, Traveler's motion fails.

Traveler's Motion to Compel Documents Responsive to its RPD No. 36 is granted in part and denied in part. it is overbroad to the extent it seeks documents which "relate" to Plaintiff's Responses to Special Interrogatories Set One. It also appears from Plaintiff's briefings here that Plaintiff has already produced any and all non-privileged responsive documents. Accordingly, Plaintiff is ordered to provide an Amended, verified, Response to RPD No. 36 preserving his objections and affirmatively stating under oath that any and all non-privileged responsive documents bearing on the information sought by Special Interrogatories Set One Nos.(5-8) have already been produced. Prevailing party to prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: David Helbraun; Clerk: William Trupek; Court Reporter: Jaime Hopp CSR#13751
